text_blocks.text: Poland's AD defences were no match for
the German attack and the situation was similar
in other European countries. Significant AA
warfare started with the Battle of Britain in the
summer of 1940. 3.7-inch HAA were to provide
the backbone of the groundbased AA defences,
were also used. The Army's Anti-aircraft command,
which was under command of the Air Defence
UK organisation, grew to 12 AA divisions in 3
AA corps. 40-mm Bofors entered service in
increasing numbers. In addition the RAF regiment
was formed in 1941 with responsibility for airfield air
defence, eventually with Bofors 40mm as their main
armament. Fixed AA defences, using HAA) and
LDA, were established by the Army in key
overseas places, notably Malta, Suez Canal and
